toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/system.text.json/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('System.Text.Json:', stable[-1])" 2>/dev/null &
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.extensions.logging/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('Microsoft.Extensions.Logging:', stable[-1])" 2>/dev/null &
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.extensions.hosting/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting:', stable[-1])" 2>/dev/null &
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/azure.storage.blobs/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('Azure.Storage.Blobs:', stable[-1])" 2>/dev/null &
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.aspnetcore.mvc.newtonsoftjson/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson (all):', [v for v in stable if v.startswith('6.')][-1], '/', [v for v in stable if v.startswith('8.')][-1])" 2>/dev/null &
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/opentelemetry.extensions.hosting/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting:', stable[-1])" 2>/dev/null &
curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/xunit/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; versions=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; stable=[v for v in versions if '-' not in v]; print('xunit:', stable[-1])" 2>/dev/null &
wait

Now I have the latest versions. Let me plan the changes:

**Latest versions:**
- System.Text.Json: 10.0.7
- Microsoft.Extensions.Logging: 10.0.7
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 10.0.7
- Azure.Storage.Blobs: 12.27.0
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson: 6.0.36 (net6.0), 8.0.26 (net8.0)
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.3


- xunit: 2.9.3 (already latest)

I need to centralize version management by defining all package versions as MSBuild properties in Directory.Build.props and using those properties consistently across the solution. The current setup already has Directory.Build.props defining some versions like BlobsVersion and HostingVersion, so I'll expand this approach to cover all packages and update them to their latest versions.

I'm realizing I need to be careful with the Legacy project since it targets net6.0—System.Text.Json 10.0 requires .NET 10 or .NET Standard 2.0, so I'll need to check compatibility before upgrading that particular package.

For the AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson package, the versions are tied to the target framework (6.0.x for net6.0, 8.0.x for net8.0), so those will need to remain conditional even though I can define them as properties. My approach is to add all version properties to Directory.Build.props, including the latest versions for the packages that support them across frameworks.

Now I'm mapping out which properties each project file will reference—Common.props gets the Logging version, Core.csproj uses the System.Text.Json and Hosting versions, Api.csproj uses System.Text.Json and the conditional NewtonsoftJson properties, Web.csproj uses System.Text.Json and OpenTelemetry.Hosting, and Legacy.csproj will use System.Text.Json and Blobs.

Version, while Tests.csproj uses Xunit and System.Text.Json. This is a clean approach, so I'll go ahead and implement it.
